codeReview.summary: The credential proxy implementation provides the core infrastructure for sandbox-external credential management, but contains a blocking bug in the scope validation logic that will cause credential requests to fail for non-read operations.

issues.description: In `request_credential()`, the validation check `if !scope.allowed_operations.contains(&"read".to_string())` rejects any scope that doesn't explicitly contain "read" operation. This breaks credential requests for git scopes (which have "push", "pull", "fetch") and LLM scopes ("chat", "embed"). The check should validate that the requested operation type matches the scope's operation_type, not that it contains a generic "read" permission.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Worker followed project conventions correctly - used async_trait, thiserror, tracing, proper error propagation with Result types.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: credential_proxy.rs uses async_trait::async_trait, thiserror::Error, tokio::sync::RwLock as specified in AGENTS.md

sharedStateObservations.observation: Worker followed Rust skill guidance properly. No deviations from recommended patterns.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Transcript shows skill was loaded and followed. All async tests use #[tokio::test], proper Arc<dyn Trait> for dynamic dispatch.

sharedStateObservations.observation: No additional services were started. Worker used only cargo build and cargo test commands which are documented in services.yaml.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Verification commands were: cargo build --workspace, cargo test -p swell-tools -- credential_proxy, cargo test --workspace - all documented in services.yaml

sharedStateObservations.observation: Worker did not discover any new codebase knowledge that should be documented. Implementation was straightforward using existing patterns.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: No time spent reverse-engineering existing code or finding documentation. All patterns followed established conventions.

summary: Feature implementation provides credential proxy infrastructure with proper sandbox boundary separation. However, the scope validation logic at line 370 contains a blocking bug that will cause credential requests to fail for non-read operation scopes (git push/pull, LLM chat/embed). The check requires "read" to be in allowed_operations, but git scopes only have push/pull/fetch and LLM scopes only have chat/embed. This needs to be fixed before the feature can be considered functional.
